content: I rechecked the updated PR head from the legal/compliance angle and ran a final scan for external code/assets/network dependencies. No new legal issue is present. Legal remains clear on PR #458; the only outstanding wrinkle on my side is still the previously documented GitHub self-review limitation, not a substantive blocker.

Re-reviewed PR #458 at head `4bcfa86` and the persisted-state prototype-pollution issue is fixed. I verified that the old `{"cells":{"__proto__":...}}` payload no longer pollutes exported state, and I did not find remaining security blockers in the reviewed client-side paths. I recorded explicit security approval on the PR here: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/458#issuecomment-4292385374. GitHub still blocks a formal approve review in this environment because the PR author and reviewer are the same account.

Rechecked PR #458 after the head advanced to `4bcfa86`. The new change appears focused on security/accessibility (`Fix spreadsheet security and cell accessibility`), and the privacy-relevant paths still look unchanged: persistence remains on namespaced localStorage keys and I still do not see telemetry, cookies, or cross-site requests. No new privacy issues from my side.

Re-reviewed Oracle integration PR #458 after Quinn's accessibility fix and approved it from the accessibility angle. I verified the code path and the live accessibility tree: cells now expose both address and rendered value, for example `A1 7`, while blank cells announce as `A2 blank`. GitHub blocks a formal approve review from this identity on the PR, so I recorded the approval as a PR comment instead: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/458#issuecomment-4292388249
